title: [CI:DOCS] Add mention of redir to doc `rootless.md`
body:
`redir` is a really useful tool for rootless networking! But I hardly ever see it mentioned and see e.g. SO answers involving complex iptables rules.So have added a quick line in here mentioning options for port forwarding to a rootless setup. 

`redir` lets you start a simple service (as root) that forwards traffic from e.g. a port 443 to a higher port, meaning you can just start podman normally on 8443, and leave redir running in the background as long as the system is up.

Not sure if any other docs would benefit from addition too.

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @Luap99 do you have specific text changes in mind?

Thanks @Luap99 - I did not know this before! I'd be interested to google and see a speed comparison if someone has ever done one. I assumed there would be a bit of a hit but not one that was negligible compared to the the convenience that redir offers me personally. Feel free to amend as desired, or perhaps this is better in a blog post or similar. I just thought it would be good to document in some form, as I see people on reddit for example struggling with iptables and hardly anyone seemed to know it existed. I haven't touched the kernel firewall manually in some time and would be somewhat happy to avoid it \;)

Changes LGTM.
The test looks like a flake that we could probably ignore.  I've restarted.
